---
title: "Tags and filters"
description: "Mark items as vegetarian, spicy, new, or chef's pick — and let diners filter the menu by tag."
sidebarTitle: "Tags and filters"
icon: "tag"
---

Tags are short labels you attach to menu items. Diners see them as
coloured pills next to each item and can filter the whole menu down to
"just the vegetarian things" or "just the new items".

## Platform presets vs your custom tags

Rumizi ships a set of curated presets — the common ones like *Vegetarian*,
*Vegan*, *Spicy*, *Gluten-free*, *New*, *Chef's pick*. These come with
consistent translations across every supported language.

You can also create **custom tags** that only exist for your venue. For
example, *"Nonna's recipe"* or *"House special"*.

<Note>
Adopting a platform preset takes a **snapshot** into your venue. If the
preset text changes later on the platform, your adopted copy doesn't
change — you keep what you picked.
</Note>

## Adding a tag to an item

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the item">
    In the menu editor **Items** tab, tap the item you want to tag.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Open the tag picker">
    Tap the tag area. You'll see your existing tags plus the platform
    presets you haven't adopted yet.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Pick or create">
    Tap an existing tag to apply it, or tap **New tag** to create a
    custom one with your own label and colour.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Save the item">
    The tag appears on the item card and, once you publish, on the diner's
    menu.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Filters on the diner side

On the public menu, every tag that has at least one tagged item becomes a
filter chip in the menu header. Diners can tap multiple chips to narrow
the menu down.

<Tip>
A minimum of three items across one or more tags is required before the
filter bar appears — a single *New* item doesn't push the whole filter UI
at diners.
</Tip>

## Custom colours and icons

Each tag can have its own colour and an optional icon. Pick colours that
are readable on both light and dark backgrounds — the menu theme colours
show through.
